comment.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [http://www.justwatch.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.justwatch.com%2F) ### Comment > Subject: Bug report: QUIC/HTTP3 connections not respected by HTTPS exclusions in Network Extension mode (Safari) > > Hello AdGuard support team, > > I'm reporting a bug in AdGuard for Mac that causes JavaScript chunk loading failures on websites when using Safari, specifically related to QUIC/HTTP3 traffic handling in Network Extension mode. > > --- > > ENVIRONMENT > - AdGuard for Mac (latest version) > - macOS with Safari (latest) > - Filtering mode: Network Extension > - HTTPS filtering: enabled > > --- > > PROBLEM SUMMARY > > When AdGuard is running, certain JavaScript chunks fail to load on justwatch.com in Safari, causing Vue.js components (search bar, login module) to not initialize. The issue does not occur in Chrome. Quitting AdGuard entirely resolves the issue immediately. > > --- > > SYMPTOMS > - Search bar does not appear in the navbar > - Login button is unresponsive or partially functional > - Elements appear after ~1 minute (seemingly due to Safari's automatic chunk retry) > - Behavior is non-deterministic — some chunks load, others fail depending on timing > > Safari DevTools Console errors observed: > • ChunkLoadError: Loading chunk npm.sentry failed > • Unhandled Promise Rejection: ChunkLoadError: Loading chunk chunk-2d0aa928 failed > • Unhandled Promise Rejection: ChunkLoadError: Loading chunk chunk-2d21061a failed > > All failing chunks are served from: https://www.justwatch.com/appassets/js/ > > The Filtering Log shows repeated QUIC connections to geo.justwatch.com:443 with no rule applied and no filtering entry, despite the domain being fully excluded. > > --- > > TROUBLESHOOTING STEPS ALREADY TAKEN > > 1. Added allowlist rules to User Rules: > `@@||justwatch.com^$important` > `@@||justwatch.com^$stealth` > `@@||geo.justwatch.com^$important` > `@@||accounts.justwatch.com^$important` > `@@||accounts.justwatch.com^$stealth` > `@@||justwatch.com^$generichide` > `@@||justwatch.com^$specifichide` > > 2. Added to HTTPS filtering exclusions: > justwatch.com > *.justwatch.com > > 3. Verified Stealth Mode settings — the $stealth rule resolved session/logout issues but did not fix chunk loading. > > 4. Disabled the AdGuard Safari extension entirely — problem persisted, confirming it is caused by the AdGuard app (network level), not the browser extension. > > 5. Checked Network → General settings — no QUIC-specific blocking option is available in the UI. > > 6. Attempted to disable HTTP/3 in Safari's Feature Flags — the option is no longer available in this Safari version, as HTTP/3 has been promoted to stable. > > --- > > ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS > > The HTTPS exclusions list appears to only apply to TLS-over-TCP traffic. QUIC (UDP-based HTTP/3) connections bypass the exclusion logic and are still intercepted or disrupted by the Network Extension tunnel. Safari defaults to HTTP/3 for supported domains and dynamically loads JavaScript chunks over QUIC. When AdGuard interferes with these QUIC connections, the chunk requests fail or time out, preventing Vue.js components from mounting. > > Chrome appears unaffected, likely because its QUIC implementation behaves differently under the Network Extension tunnel or falls back to HTTP/2 more readily. > > --- > > EXPECTED BEHAVIOR > > Domains listed in the HTTPS exclusions (including wildcard *.justwatch.com) should have ALL their encrypted traffic excluded from AdGuard's inspection and interception, regardless of whether the connection uses TLS over TCP or QUIC over UDP. > > --- > > Please let me know if you need additional logs or information. I can provide a HAR file or network traces if helpful. > > Thank you. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/jzzd2bizdtsw44g4w4sok0gw4co8wkor490iwreck0.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/3tcpiqfph1a84wcscskg4ggsgswk0cshcuu525x2q0.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 3 ![Screenshot 3](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/9vmrri78wdss40k4go0cwco4g0okws4jpk6qhgob40.jpg?nc=1)
